_comment: Game content for the 4 rounds. Hard/obscure by design; question text never names its own answer. Round 2 photos load live from Wikipedia via 'wiki'. Round 3 'audioUrl' is null because famous broadcast calls are copyrighted — plug in licensed clips or read the transcript aloud. Round 4 draws its draft pool from legends.json. Extend with tools/fetch_content.py.

questions.q: On April 6, 1973, who took the first plate appearance as a designated hitter in Major League history?
questions.fact: Ron Blomberg walked with the bases loaded off Luis Tiant in Boston.

questions.q: Which of these pitchers did NOT throw a perfect game in pinstripes?
questions.fact: Larsen (1956), Wells (1998) and Cone (1999) were perfect; Guidry never was.

questions.q: Which reliever became the first in the American League to win a Cy Young Award, taking it in 1977?
questions.fact: Sparky Lyle went 13-5 with 26 saves out of the bullpen.

questions.q: Which Yankee was named American League MVP in 1950?
questions.fact: The Scooter hit .324 and scored 125 runs at shortstop.

questions.q: Who managed the Yankees to their first World Series championship, in 1923?
questions.fact: Huggins led the franchise to its first title over the crosstown Giants.

questions.q: When Lou Gehrig pulled himself from the lineup in 1939, which first baseman replaced him?
questions.fact: Babe Dahlgren ended the 2,130-game streak by starting at first base.

questions.q: The Yankees retired uniform number 8 for which pair of players?
questions.fact: Both were catchers — Dickey mentored Berra, who inherited both the position and the number.

questions.q: Who holds the Yankees' single-season record for runs batted in?
questions.fact: Gehrig drove in 185 in 1931 — still the American League record.
The homegrown Oklahoman was billed as the heir to Mickey Mantle.
A switch-hitting left fielder who played his entire career (1965–79) in the Bronx.
The ace of the lean 1960s later became a celebrated pitching coach.
“Old Reliable” was known for coming through in the clutch beside DiMaggio.
The mustachioed lefty later wrote the tell-all diary ‘The Bronx Zoo.’
The second baseman is the only World Series MVP ever chosen from the losing team (1960).

questions.prompt: Identify the player and the moment
questions.transcript: "Deep to left... Yastrzemski will not get it... it's a three-run homer, and the Yankees have taken the lead at Fenway!"
questions.fact: A light-hitting shortstop's three-run shot over the Green Monster decided the one-game playoff.

questions.transcript: "Leading off the eleventh... he swings at the very first pitch... deep to left field, and it is GONE — the Yankees win the pennant!"
questions.fact: He turned on Tim Wakefield's first knuckleball of the inning to sink the rival Red Sox.

questions.prompt: Name the pitcher and the game
questions.transcript: "Strike three called! It's a perfect game — and the catcher runs out and leaps into his arms!"
questions.fact: The only perfect game in World Series history — Yogi Berra jumped into his arms.

questions.transcript: "On the very first pitch... driven deep to center field... that is three home runs, on three swings, tonight!"
questions.fact: Three swings, three homers off three different pitchers in the Game 6 clincher — Mr. October.

questions.prompt: Which Series-turning home run is this?
questions.transcript: "Three-and-two... swung on and drilled to left... it is GONE! A game-tying three-run homer in the eighth!"
questions.fact: His homer off Mark Wohlers tied Game 4 in Atlanta and swung the whole 1996 Series.

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Red Sox never sold Babe Ruth to the Yankees in 1919?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: how many of the Yankees' 27 titles still happen?
options: About the same — the dynasty finds another way
Very few — Ruth was the spark
Zero — no Curse of the Bambino, no dynasty

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Yankees had kept a young Jay Buhner instead of trading him for Ken Phelps in 1988?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: how much does that change the early-'90s rebuild?
options: Not much — the Core Four still carries it
A little — a better bridge to 1996
A lot — a franchise cornerstone lost

whatIfs.scenario: What if Mickey Mantle had stayed fully healthy his entire career?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: where does he rank all-time?
options: Elite, but roughly the same legacy

whatIfs.scenario: What if the Yankees had drafted and kept a prime two-way Shohei Ohtani?
whatIfs.prompt: Vote: how many titles in the next five years?
